sections.paragraph: (contr. from מָה יָדוּעַ what is taught? i.q. for what reason? Gr. τί μαθών), adv. of interrogation, why, wherefore? Jos 17:14 2Sa 19:42 used in an oblique interrogation, Exo 3:3.- Job 21:4, the words אִם מַדּוּעַ are not to be taken as closely connected; for אִם as is very often the case (see אִם B, 1 ) answers to הֲ interrogative in the former member, and the entire verse is to be rendered as containing a double inquiry, “do I complain of man, וְאִם מַדּוּעַ לֹא תִקְצַר מוּתִי or why should I not be impatient?” oder weshalb foll ich nicht ungeduldig werden?
